input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
## .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

### 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically with the Android workload, or manually from [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Managed via the MAUI workload or [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) | Emulators, platform APIs |

### 2. Step-by-Step Installation

```bash
# 1 — Xcode (after installing from App Store)
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# 2 — .NET SDK (grab the latest LTS or STS from dot.net)
#     On Apple Silicon, pick the Arm64 installer.
#     Verify:
dotnet --version

# 3 — MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui

# 4 — (Optional) Android Studio for emulator management
#     Or let the MAUI workload handle Android SDK/NDK.
```

### 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See which .NET SDKs you have
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads and available updates
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# Check which Android SDK components were installed
dotnet android sdk list          # MAUI's built-in manager

# Check Xcode version & active developer directory
xcodebuild -version
xcode-select -p
```

Cross-reference the **MAUI release notes** for version compatibility:
→ [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases)

### 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios --project HelloMaui

# Run on Android Emulator (must have one created)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android --project HelloMaui
```

### 5. Common Gotchas

- **Apple Silicon**: use the **Arm64** .NET installer — the x64 build works under Rosetta but is slower.
- **iOS provisioning**: for physical devices you need an Apple Developer account and a signing profile configured in your `.csproj` (`<CodesignKey>`, `<CodesignProvision>`).
- **Android emulator on Apple Silicon**: use the **arm64-v8a** system images, not x86_64.
- **`xcode-select -p`** must point to the full Xcode app (not just CommandLineTools) for iOS builds to succeed.

### 6. Recommended IDE

- **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — the primary supported editor on macOS going forward.

That covers the full path from a clean machine to a building & running MAUI app on both platforms.
